items.title: Senior Canadian commander charged by military police in compensation probe
items.summary_auto: Canada's military police charged Col. James Smith with prejudice to good order regarding compensation irregularities while he commanded Task Force Latvia. Legal proceedings are pending.

items.title: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician, diplomat and journalist, dead at 88
New trial ordered for man found guilty of murder in Quebec City Halloween sword attacks
items.summary_auto: In a decision rendered Tuesday, the Quebec Court of Appeal has ordered a new trial for Carl Girouard, the man who was found guilty of first-degree murder following the Quebec City sword attacks on Halloween night in 2020.

items.title: Woman killed after being hit by train in Montreal's Mile End neighbourhood
items.summary_auto: Montreal police are investigating the death of a 26-year-old woman who was hit by a train in Montreal's Mile End neighbourhood Tuesday morning.

items.title: Nowhere is safe from the wrath of a Canada goose — not even this Ontario campus
items.summary_auto: Last week, a Canada goose found its way into a building at Wilfrid Laurier University's Waterloo, Ont., campus and was captured by several witnesses on video trying to fly away, causing students to run for cover. The goose's capers went viral on social media.

items.title: Carney announces $3.8B to protect nature, new conservation sites in James Bay and Manitoba
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced $3.8 billion in new funding to protect nature on Tuesday, as part of the federal government’s goal to protect 30 per cent of Canada's lands and waters by 2030.

items.title: Fans were sold a magical Barbie Dream Fest. Instead, they got cardboard cutouts and hand sanitizer
items.summary_auto: Add the three-day Barbie Dream Fest in Fort Lauderdale to the list of highly marketed conventions that didn't live up to the hype.

items.title: Get excited: How and where to watch humans go back to the moon
items.summary_auto: Your go-to guide for CBC News coverage of the Artemis II mission around the moon.

items.title: Tiger Woods had pills in his pocket at crash scene and bloodshot eyes, police say
items.summary_auto: Tiger Woods's eyes were bloodshot and glassy, his pupils dilated, and he had hydrocodone pills in his pocket when interviewed at the scene of his car crash last week in Florida, according to a sheriff's office report released Tuesday.

items.title: Just For Laughs founder Gilbert Rozon ordered to pay $880K to 8 plaintiffs in sex-assault civil trial
items.summary_auto: A Quebec Superior Court judge has ordered Gilbert Rozon, the Just For Laughs founder, to pay just under a little more than $880,000 to eight of the nine women who have accused him of sexual assault and misconduct in a civil trial.

items.title: Eurovision to expand with Asian edition later this year
items.summary_auto: The music spectacle Eurovision will hold its first Asian edition in Bangkok later this year.

items.title: Murder victim found in Quebec decades ago identified as missing Ontario woman
items.summary_auto: Police say genetic testing has been used to confirm a homicide victim found in Quebec in 1979 was a woman who had been reported missing months earlier from Ontario.

items.title: Sexual assault case against Frank Stronach built on fabricated and unreliable evidence: defence
items.summary_auto: The women who testified they were sexually assaulted by Frank Stronach all failed "quite miserably" on credibility and reliability,  meaning the court should find the Canadian businessman not guilty, his lawyer argued on Tuesday.

items.title: Canada's economy saw slight growth in January with gains in mining, oil and gas
items.summary_auto: Canada's economy saw slight growth in January, as gains in goods-producing industries like mining offset a slowdown in manufacturing, Statistics Canada said on Tuesday.

items.title: King Charles to make first state visit to U.S.
items.summary_auto: King Charles and Queen Camilla will make a state visit to the United States this spring, Buckingham Palace has announced.

items.title: UCP members express love for Canada as Alberta NDP raises questions about op-ed on referendum petition
items.summary_auto: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith and her United Conservative government spoke of their unwavering love for Canada on Monday as the Opposition NDP accused them of harbouring a separatist.

items.title: The premier called a gas-tax holiday 'the most important thing' Manitoba's ever done. Now, he's being called out
items.summary_auto: Premier Wab Kinew says his government's gas tax holiday was the most important thing a Manitoba government has ever done. In 2024, Kinew's NDP government suspended the 14 cents per litre gas tax for an entire year, a move that cost the province $340 million in tax revenue.

items.title: Weather watchers mourn the end of national Weatheradio service
items.summary_auto: There is now one less way to get weather information in Canada. Environment and Climate Change Canada is ending its long-running Weatheradio service. It comes after ECCC closed many of its weather accounts on social media specific to provinces and territories.

items.title: Robert Pattinson, Zendaya's The Drama is disturbing, controversial and fascinating
items.summary_auto: That The Drama blossoms into a subversive investigation into the limits of empathy doesn't really matter. All that will matter is the disappointment theatre-goers will feel walking out of a supposed rom-com they expected to be like Sleepless in Seattle, but that instead feels closer to Midsommar.

items.title: Why are bags of Big Mac sauce washing up along the Bay of Fundy?
items.summary_auto: Empty bags of Big Mac and McChicken sauce have been washing up along the Bay of Fundy recently. And local residents are not lovin’ it.

items.title: U.S. strikes city home to Iranian nuclear site, Trump urges allies to help in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: The United States hit the central Iranian city of Isfahan early Tuesday, sending a massive fireball into the sky, and Tehran struck a fully loaded Kuwaiti oil tanker in the Persian Gulf.

items.title: Have seniors become the new screenagers?
items.summary_auto: Sherry Bagnato isn’t a millennial or a screenager. She’s a 67-year-old retired Toronto mother of two, and her phone habits have flipped the script at home. Now, she admits, it’s her adult children telling her to put the device down.

items.title: Weeks after Ontario dental clinic was ordered shut, patients sent letters urging hepatitis and HIV testing
items.summary_auto: Former patients of a dental clinic in Brantford, Ont., are being warned by Grand Erie Public Health to get tested for hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV due to an infection prevention and control lapse investigation.

items.title: CTA to lower air passenger complaints fee after pressure from airlines, transport minister, documents show
items.summary_auto: Internal documents show airlines and then Transport Minister Anita Anand pressed the Canadian Transportation Agency to scale back a proposed complaints fee — raising concerns about political interference.

items.title: Public servant says she's 'collateral damage' as government prepares to test new pay system
items.summary_auto: A longtime federal public servant says she’s become "collateral damage" as her department clears its backlog of Phoenix payroll issues so it can test replacement software — and in doing so introduced an error to her file, refused to fix it and is now clawing back hundreds of dollars per paycheque.

items.title: Her scammer only got house arrest. She says Canada's sentences are a 'joke'
items.summary_auto: Scam victims feel an increasing sense that the Canadian justice system is overly lenient on the people who took advantage of them.

items.title: Most people across Canada will no longer need to pay nurse practitioners for primary care
items.summary_auto: As of Wednesday, the federal government will require provinces and territories to start to pay for primary care services, including those provided by nurse practitioners. The new policy aims to ensure Canadians aren't being billed for "medically necessary services."

items.title: Inquest begins into death of 24-year-old Indigenous woman who died at Niagara, Ont., hospital
items.summary_auto: A long-awaited coroner's inquest into the 2021 death of Heather Winterstein at a hospital in St. Catharines, Ont., got underway on Monday, with Winterstein's mother telling the five-person jury about the torment she feels over her daughter's death.

items.title: Patients of Ontario dental clinic speak out after hepatitis, HIV tests urged due to sterilization lapses
items.summary_auto: Former patients of a dental clinic in Brantford, Ont., are being warned by Grand Erie Public Health to get tested for hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV due to an infection prevention and control lapse investigation.

items.title: Trump is considering U.S. ground troops in Iran. Here are his options
items.summary_auto: As U.S. President Donald Trump considers ordering U.S. ground troops into Iran, military experts say trying to capture any of the most likely targets would pose a significant challenge.

items.title: Heat dome burns off mountain snow in western U.S.,  flashing warning for fire season
items.summary_auto: Mountains that normally see their peak snowpack in March are brown this year, thanks to a spring heat dome that baked western U.S. for much of the second half of the month. That's raising alarm bells for the fire season that's already ramping up.

items.title: Ottawa authorizes emergency use of strychnine in Alberta, Saskatchewan to address gopher infestation
items.summary_auto: Ottawa is authorizing the controlled and time-limited emergency use of strychnine to address millions of dollars worth of damage in Alberta and Saskatchewan caused by an infestation of Richardson's ground squirrels, known colloquially as gophers.

items.title: Toronto Maple Leafs fire general manager Brad Treliving
items.summary_auto: Maple Leaf Sports and Entertainment have decided to part ways with Toronto Maple Leafs General Manager Brad Treliving.

items.title: Alberta pushing for constitutional change to give provinces say over appointment of judges
items.summary_auto: Alberta's government is calling on Ottawa to change the Constitution to give provinces more of a say in how federal superior court judges are appointed at the provincial level.

items.title: B.C. youth charged after chats with U.S. teen who allegedly made school shooting threats: police
items.summary_auto: A Sooke, B.C., youth has been arrested and charged after online chats with a U.S. teenager who allegedly made school shooting threats, RCMP say.
